top_categories.workout_context: 12,651ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
112ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Eroica Japan, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Eroica Japan's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
At mile 60 of 113, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Eroica Japan's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 113 miles.
12,651ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Eroica Japan. Train it until it feels boring.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 113 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Low-cadence force work for 12,651ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 12,651ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Eroica Japan, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Eroica Japan’s 113 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Eroica Japan with 12,651ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Eroica Japan requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, TT Threshold, and Over Under to prepare you for 113 miles of mixed terrain in Kitakaruizawa, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.
